title: eremolaena humblotiana
intro: Eremolaena humblotiana, commonly known as the "Dahana" or "Tulear Spiny Head", is a species of flowering plant in the family Sarcolaenaceae, native to the island of Madagascar. This plant is traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial activities. The health benefits of Eremolaena humblotiana include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat various skin conditions such as wounds, eczema, and acne. In traditional Malagasy medicine, the plant is prepared as a tea or infusions, and its leaves and roots are also used in herbal preparations, including tinctures and poultices.
